content:
Can a Registered Will still be rejected by a court?
Yes. , In a significant judgment, the Supreme Court has reaffirmed that registration alone does not prove the genuineness of a Will. Where suspicious circumstances surround its execution, the burden lies on the propounder to remove those doubts and satisfy the judicial conscience of the Court.

This ruling is an important precedent for matters involving:
• Will disputes
• Inheritance and succession
• Probate proceedings
• Property litigation
A valuable judgment for advocates, law students, and anyone dealing with succession law.
